section3_router_structure.title: (3) React Router Structure — Required Changes
current_router_issues: TicketDetailPage at fixed '/4' — cannot pass ticketId
All 17 pages are eagerly imported (no lazy loading)
routesMeta in router.tsx duplicates navGroups in Sidebar.tsx

key_changes.reason: Ticket creation page with URL params
Dynamic route for ticket detail. useParams() to get ticketId.

target_router.route_order_critical: tickets/new MUST be before tickets/:ticketId. Otherwise 'new' is parsed as a ticketId parameter.

breadcrumb_enhancement.current: Header.tsx uses routesMeta.find(r => r.path === pathname) — exact match only
breadcrumb_enhancement.problem: /tickets/2641 does not match any routesMeta path. Breadcrumb shows fallback '대시보드'.
breadcrumb_enhancement.solution: Enhance breadcrumb to handle dynamic routes. For /tickets/:ticketId, show: '홈 > A/S 티켓 목록 > AS-2641'. For /1?phone=..., show: '홈 > 고객 정보 조회 > 김민수'.
breadcrumb_enhancement.implementation: Pattern match: if pathname.startsWith('/tickets/') and has segment after → ticket detail breadcrumb. Use ticket data from react-query cache for ticket number display.

section4_state_management.title: (4) State Management Strategy
section4_state_management.decision: zustand (client state) + @tanstack/react-query (server state). NOT Redux, NOT Context.

vs_redux: Redux is overkill for 12 users + 15 pages. zustand is 1.1KB with zero boilerplate.
This app's state is 95% server state (tickets, customers, costs) → react-query handles it.
Client state is minimal: auth user, sidebar toggle, toast queue.
Redux DevTools equivalent exists for both zustand (zustand/middleware) and react-query (ReactQueryDevtools).
vs_context: Context causes unnecessary re-renders when any part of context changes.
Multiple contexts (AuthContext, UIContext, ...) quickly becomes context hell.
zustand's selector pattern (useStore(s => s.field)) only re-renders when selected field changes.
Context doesn't persist across page reloads. zustand persist middleware does.

uiStore.note: Replaces AppLayout's useState(sidebarOpen). Shared across components without prop drilling.

server_state_via_react_query.principle: All data from API is managed by react-query. No zustand for server data.
benefits: Automatic caching (staleTime 30s → same ticket detail not refetched within 30s)
Background refetch on window focus (disabled for this app)
Mutation + invalidation (create ticket → invalidate ticket list cache)
Loading/error states built-in (no manual isLoading/error in stores)

navigation_caching.scenario: User on CustomerPage(phone=010-1234-5678) → clicks ticket AS-2641 → TicketDetailPage → clicks back → CustomerPage
behavior: 1. CustomerPage fetches customer data: queryKey ['customer', 'phone', '010-1234-5678'] → cached
5. User clicks back button → browser navigates to /1?phone=010-1234-5678
6. CustomerPage remounts → react-query checks cache → staleTime not expired → INSTANT display (no loading spinner)
7. If staleTime expired → shows cached data immediately + background refetch → updates if changed
navigation_caching.result: Back navigation is INSTANT. No re-fetch. No loading flash.

cross_page_data_sharing.pattern: URL params + react-query cache. NOT global store.

examples.why_not_store: URL params are shareable, bookmarkable, survive page refresh. Store state is lost on refresh.

examples.react_query_bonus: If customer was already fetched by TicketDetailPage's CustomerInfoCard, react-query cache has ['customer', 'phone', '010-1234-5678'] → CustomerPage instant.

section5_api_call_flow_and_caching.title: (5) API Call Flow & Caching Strategy

ticket_keys: ['tickets', { scope, status, page, sort }]  → ticket list
consultation_keys: ['consultations', { keyword, channel, type, agent, from, to, page }]  → consultation list

defaults.gcTime: 300_000 (5min) — cached data kept for 5min after unmount

customer_by_phone.reason: Customer data changes rarely. Longer cache for instant back-nav.

ticket_detail.reason: Ticket status changes frequently. Shorter cache.

purchase_history.reason: Purchase data rarely changes. Aggressive caching for tab switch performance.

create_ticket.note: Customer's AS history tab shows new ticket immediately after creation

send_message.note: Email/SMS send creates MailLog + TicketEvent → both consultation list and ticket timeline need refresh

navigation_api_flow_diagram: → staleTime 60s not expired → INSTANT render from cache
→ NO API call, NO loading spinner

challenges.challenge: Header breadcrumb breaks for dynamic routes
challenges.detail: routesMeta.find(r => r.path === pathname) fails for /tickets/2641 because no routesMeta entry has path '/tickets/2641'. Returns fallback dashboard.

implementation: if (staticMatch) return [{ label: staticMatch.title, icon: staticMatch.icon }];
// Try to get ticket number from react-query cache

challenges.challenge: URL state vs component state conflict
challenges.detail: CustomerPage uses ?phone= URL param. If user searches phone A, navigates to ticket, comes back — URL still has phone A. But if user then searches phone B without navigating away, URL updates. What if they go back again?

solution.approach: useSearchParams() as single source of truth
rules: CustomerPage reads phone from useSearchParams(), not from component state.
Search action: setSearchParams({ phone: newPhone }) → URL updates → react-query refetches.
Browser back: URL reverts → component re-reads from URL → cache hit → instant.
No duplicate state: phone exists only in URL, never in useState or zustand.

challenges.challenge: TicketCreatePage needs context from multiple origins
challenges.detail: TicketCreatePage can be reached from: CustomerPage (+A/S button), ConsultationPage (+A/S button), TicketListPage (+New button), Header quick action. Each passes different URL params.

solution.approach: URL search params with graceful degradation
solution.behavior: TicketCreatePage reads all params. If phone present → auto-search customer. If orderId → auto-fill product. If none → blank form.

challenges.challenge: Sidebar active state for dynamic routes
challenges.detail: Sidebar uses NavLink with exact path matching. /tickets/2641 doesn't match '/tickets' so neither the list nor detail sidebar item highlights.

solution.approach: NavLink with custom isActive or startsWith matching
solution.note: Only apply end={false} to routes with children (/tickets). Other routes like /1, /6 keep end={true} (default).

challenges.challenge: react-query cache prefetching for navigation performance
challenges.detail: When user hovers over a ticket row in CustomerPage's A/S이력 tab, we can prefetch that ticket's detail. So when they click, it's instant.

solution.trigger: onMouseEnter on table row (desktop only, not mobile)
solution.benefit: Ticket detail page loads instantly (0ms) when user clicks — data already in cache.

challenges.detail: User scrolls down CustomerPage purchase list, clicks ticket, goes to TicketDetailPage, presses back. Page re-renders but scroll position is lost (top of page).

solution.note: ScrollRestoration auto-saves scroll position per route. On back navigation, restores scroll. Built into react-router v7.
solution.fallback: If ScrollRestoration doesn't work with AppLayout's overflow-y-auto (main-content div), use manual scroll save in sessionStorage per route.
